title: Hemionitis regnelliana
intro: Hemionitis regnelliana, also known as the Brazilian fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis regnelliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is used to support immune function, lower blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Hemionitis regnelliana is classified under the family Polypodiaceae. side_effects: Hemionitis regnelliana, also known as the Rengel's oak fern, is a plant that may have potential side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains compounds such as thiaminase, which can break down thiamine in the body and cause thiamine deficiency if ingested.
